(Toronto Wolfpack) 27 | top try scorer_link = |prevseason_link= 2016 League 1 |prevseason_year=2016 |nextseason_link= 2018 RFL League 1 |nextseason_year=2018 }} The '''2017 League 1''', known as the '''Kingstone Press League 1''' for sponsorship reasons, was a professional [[rugby league|rugby league football]] competition played in England, Wales and Canada, the third tier of the sport for RFL affiliated clubs. The 2017 League 1 season was expanded to 16 teams with the first professional Canadian team [[Toronto Wolfpack]] joining the British game. The fixture list was first issued in October 2016 when the inclusion of [[York City Knights]] in the league was in doubt so the Knights were omitted from the fixture list.{{cite news |title=The 2017 season begins here |newspaper=[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]] |date=24 October 2016 |page=13 |issue=3042}} York's inclusion in the league was confirmed at the beginning of December and the fixture list was re-issued to include the club.{{cite news |url=http://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/14945688.On_the_plane_again__York_City_Knights__39__2017_League_One_fixture_list_confirmed |title=On the plane again! York City Knights' 2017 League One fixture list confirmed |newspaper=[[The Press (York)|The Press]] |date=4 December 2016 |accessdate=21 December 2016}} The format of the season was a regular season of 15 games where every team played each other once. After 15 games the top eight teams played each other once more in the League 1 Super 8s. The team finishing top of the Super 8s won automatic promotion to the [[2018 RFL Championship|2018 Championship]] and named league champions for 2017. The teams finishing second to fifth met in two play-off semi-finals with the semi-final winners meeting in the promotion play-off final. The league title was won by Toronto who only dropped three points all season (a defeat to York and a draw with [[Keighley Cougars|Keighley]] in the Super 8s) and had a 100% winning record at home. The other promotion place was taken by [[Barrow Raiders]] who defeated Cumbrian rivals, [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] in the promotion play-off final. The clubs finishing in the lower half of the table after the regular season played in the League 1 Shield Super 8s with the top two clubs competing in the League 1 Shield final. The Cumbrian clubs, Whitehaven and Barrow finished second and third respectively. Whitehaven put a run of 12 straight wins together while Barrow who had been second for a long time lost two games towards the end of the season. York whose existence was in doubt before the season started finished fourth with Doncaster and Newcastle behind them. The last two places in the Super 8s were contested right up until the last fixture of the season with five clubs; Workington, Keighley, North Wales Crusaders, Hunslet and London Skolars all in contention. In the end the places were taken by Keighley and Workington while North Wales lost out on points difference. ===Results=== {{main|2017 League 1 results#Regular season|l1=2017 League 1 regular season results}} ===Final standings===

==Super 8s== The top eight teams after the regular season went forward to the Super 8s. Points gained in the regular season were carried forward to the Super 8s during which the clubs played each other once more. The top four clubs at entry to the Super 8s played four home fixtures and three away while the bottom four clubs had three home fixtures and four away. After all seven rounds had been played, Toronto Wolfpack were named League Champions and were automatically promoted to the Championship for 2018. Barrow Raiders, Whitehaven, York City Knights and Newcastle Thunder made the play off semi-finals. Toronto secured the automatic promotion place after round six despite having suffered their first ever league defeat in round two at York and also dropping a point at Keighley in round three. Barrow and Whitehaven met in an all-Cumbrian play-off final with Barrow winning 10–6 to secure promotion to the Championship for 2018. ===Results=== {{main|2017 League 1 results#Super 8s|l1=2017 |title=Kingstone Press League 1 Promotion final |newspaper=[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]] |page=39 |issue=3090 |date=2 October 2017}} |} ==League 1 Shield== The teams finishing in the bottom eight at the end of the regular season competed for the [[League 1 Shield]]. Points gained in the regular season were carried forward to the Shield competition. The teams played each other once more and after the completion of the seven rounds the top two teams played for the League 1 Shield. The final was contested by Hunslet and London Skolars after both teams won six of their seven games in the qualifying section. Hunlset won the trophy beating London 32–12 in the final. ===Results=== {{main|2017 League 1 results#Shield|l1=2017 League 1 Shield results}} ===Final standings=== {{#invoke:Sports table|main|style=WDL |section=Shield |show_limit=5 |for_against_style=points |winpoints=2 |start_date=30 July 2017 |team1=HUN |team2=LON |team3=CRU |team4=GLO |team5=OXF |team6=COV |team7=HEM |team8=SWI |source= [https://web.archive.org/web/20171030115039/http://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/league-one/table] |update=complete |win_COV=5 |draw_COV=0 |loss_COV=17 |gf_COV=472 |ga_COV=837 |win_GLO=9 |draw_GLO=0 |loss_GLO=13 |gf_GLO=478 |ga_GLO=702 |win_HEM=3 |draw_HEM=0 |loss_HEM=19 |gf_HEM=393 |ga_HEM=1033 |win_HUN=13 |draw_HUN=0 |loss_HUN=9 |gf_HUN=696 |ga_HUN=470 |win_LON=12 |draw_LON=1 |loss_LON=9 |gf_LON=625 |ga_LON=529 |win_CRU=11 |draw_CRU=1 |loss_CRU=10 |gf_CRU=556 |ga_CRU=574 |win_OXF=6 |draw_OXF=1 |loss_OXF=15 |gf_OXF=393 |ga_OXF=849 |win_SWI=2 |draw_SWI=1 |loss_SWI=19 |gf_SWI=299 |ga_SWI=926 |name_COV={{leagueicon|coventry|12}} [[Coventry Bears]]|status_COV= |name_GLO={{leagueicon|gloucester|12}} [[Gloucestershire All Golds]]|status_GLO= |name_HEM={{leagueicon|hemel|12}} [[Hemel Stags]]|status_HEM= |name_HUN={{leagueicon|hunslet|12}} [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]]|status_HUN= |name_LON={{leagueicon|london skolars|12}} [[London Skolars]] |status_LON= |name_CRU={{leagueicon|north wales|12}} [[North Wales Crusaders]]|status_CRU= |name_OXF={{leagueicon|oxford|12}} [[Oxford Rugby League|Oxford]]|status_OXF= |name_SWI={{leagueicon|south wales scorpions|12}} [[South Wales Ironmen]]|status_SWI= |result1=FIN |result2=FIN |res_col_header=Q |col_FIN=green1 |text_FIN=League 1 Shield final }} ===Final=== {| border=1 style="border-collapse:collapse; font-size:85%; text-align:center;" cellpadding=3 cellspacing=0 width=100% |- bgcolor=#C1D8FF !rowspan=2 width=19%|Home !rowspan=2 width=6%|Score !rowspan=2 width=19%|Away !colspan=6|Match Information |- bgcolor=#CCCCCC !width=17%|Date and Time !width=22%|Venue !width=10%|Referee !width=7%|Attendance |- bgcolor=#F5FAFF |{{leagueicon|hunslet|12}} [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] |32–12 |{{leagueicon|london skolars|12}} [[London Skolars]] |24 September, 15:00 |[[South Leeds Stadium]] |N Bennett |516 |- ! colspan=12 | Source:{{cite news |title=Kingstone Press League 1 Shield final |newspaper=[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]] |page=39 |issue=3089 |date=25 September 2017}} |} ==Awards== At the Kingstone Press Championship & League 1 awards, Toronto captain [[Craig Hall (rugby league, born 1988)|Craig Hall]] was named League 1 player of the year with the young player award going to Lewis Young of Newcastle Thunder while [[Carl Forster]] of Whitehaven was named coach of the year.{{cite web |url=http://www.totalrl.com/lunt-picks-up-major-prize-at-kingstone-press-awards-evening/ |title=Lunt picks up major prize at Kingstone Press awards evening |publisher=League Publications |date=19 September 2017 |accessdate=1 October 2017}} Hall was also the league's top goal kicker and points scorer kicking 170 goals and scoring 24 tries for a total of 436 points. His Toronto teammate, [[Liam Kay]] was the top try scorer with 27. ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://web.archive.org/web/20160426020329/http://www.rugby-league.com/kingstone_press_leagues Official Website] {{2017 in rugby league}} {{Championship 1}} [[Category:RFL League One]] [[Category:2017 in English rugby league]] [[Category:2017 in Welsh rugby league]] [[Category:2017 in Canadian rugby league]]
